sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state and try moving in different directions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find player
player_pos = None
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        player_pos = n['boundary'][0]  # [row, col]
        break
print(f"Player at: {player_pos}")

# Check what's adjacent to player
print(f"\nAdjacency list:")
for adj in seg['adjacency_list']:
    # Check if either node is the white player
    for nid in adj:
        if nodes[nid]['color'] == 'W':
            other = adj[1] if adj[0] == nid else adj[0]
            print(f"  Player adjacent to: id={other} color={nodes[other]['color']} boundary={nodes[other]['boundary']}")

# Let me try moving LEFT to see if player can move horizontally
# First, let me understand the cross structure at player's row
print(f"\nBlue cross horizontal arm at row 45:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and 'boundary' in dir(n):
        b = n['boundary']
        # Check if any boundary point is at row 45
        for pt in b:
            if pt[0] == 45:
                print(f"  Blue node id={n['id']} boundary={b}")
                break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
